turns.content: yo i cant sleep and its not because of the coffee this time im actually stressing about something
What's going on? Sometimes it helps to talk it out — what's keeping you up?
so my cdl medical card expires march 28 and i gotta go get the physical done and i know that sounds like no big deal just go to the doctor but im worried because of my cholesterol situation you know
Ah, I see — so you're worried that high cholesterol might cause issues with your medical recertification? That's a legitimate concern. What happens if the doctor flags something during the physical?

so if they flag you for something they can give you a shorter certification like instead of 2 years you get 1 year or even 6 months which means more appointments more fees more stress or in the worst case they can say you need to get it under control before they clear you and then you cant drive and if you cant drive you cant eat you know thats my whole income
That's a lot of pressure. Your livelihood literally depends on passing this physical. So your cholesterol has been high — do you know where your numbers were at last time?

last time the doctor told me 267 total which is high and he put me on atorvastatin 40mg and i been taking it but honestly not consistently like i would forget for days at a time or run out and not refill it right away because im on the road and finding a pharmacy when youre driving through rural mississippi at 11pm is not exactly easy you know

267 is definitely elevated — normal total cholesterol is under 200, and above 240 is considered high risk. But the good news is that atorvastatin at 40mg is a strong dose and can bring numbers down significantly, even with inconsistent use.

The question is whether it's brought yours down enough. Have you been taking it more regularly lately?

yeah so this month i been really good about it like every single day i set an alarm on my phone for 9pm and i take it no matter what and i been trying to eat a little better too like getting grilled chicken instead of fried when i can and drinking water instead of mountain dew which is hard because mountain dew is basically my blood at this point but im trying

That's great that you've been consistent this month! The statin needs time to work though — significant changes in cholesterol usually take 4-6 weeks of consistent use to really show up in bloodwork. When is your physical scheduled exactly?

march 28 which is like 16 days from now and i know a month of being good probably isnt enough to fix years of eating truck stop food and forgetting my pills but i gotta try right i was listening to a podcast about like biohacking and they were talking about how oatmeal and fish oil can lower cholesterol fast and i bought some of those fish oil pills at a walmart in georgia and some instant oatmeal packets that i can make in the microwave in my sleeper

Every bit helps! Fish oil and oatmeal are both legitimately good for cholesterol — the soluble fiber in oatmeal and the omega-3s in fish oil can make a modest dent. Combined with consistent statin use and better food choices, you're doing everything you can in 16 days.

Realistically though, even if your numbers are still elevated, cholesterol alone usually doesn't disqualify you from a CDL medical card. It's more about uncontrolled conditions — if you can show you're on medication and managing it, most examiners will still certify you. They might just give you a shorter card.

see thats what i needed to hear because in my head i was building this up like theyre going to take my card away and im done but a shorter certification i can deal with you know thats just more appointments which is annoying but at least im still driving

Exactly — worst realistic case is a 1-year card instead of a 2-year card, which just means coming back sooner for another check. It's an inconvenience, not a career-ender. And if you keep up with the statins consistently, your numbers should be much better by the next check.

Do you have a specific examiner or clinic you go to for the physical?

theres a dot certified place near the terminal in atlanta that a lot of drivers use and theyre pretty chill like they know we eat bad and dont exercise enough because we sit in a truck all day so they work with you on that stuff but i still get nervous every time because its like a test you know and the stakes are your whole career and i start thinking what if the blood pressure is high too because i was stressed the night before and then the stress about the test makes the numbers worse and its a whole cycle

The white-coat anxiety is real — stress about the exam can genuinely raise your blood pressure during the visit. A few tips for the day of:

- Avoid caffeine the morning of the exam
- Take deep breaths in the waiting room
- Ask them to take your blood pressure twice if the first reading is high — the second one is usually lower
- Make sure you've taken your statin the night before

The fact that the clinic near your terminal specializes in DOT physicals is a good sign. They deal with truckers all day and understand the lifestyle.

yeah good call on the caffeine thing because normally i drink like 3 cups before noon and that probably doesnt help alright so no coffee that morning take my pill the night before eat some oatmeal maybe and just go in there and be honest about what im doing to manage it right because i am doing something now even if i wasnt before

That's the right approach. Doctors appreciate honesty, and showing that you've made recent changes — consistent medication, dietary improvements — demonstrates that you're taking it seriously. They want to see effort and a plan, not perfection.

You've got 16 days of consistent meds under your belt by then, plus the dietary changes. That's a real story of someone who's actively managing their health.

alright man i feel a little better about it now not great but better and i think what was really getting to me is just the idea that somebody could take this away from me you know like driving is everything to me its how i pay for naomi its how i save for my future its literally who i am and the thought of losing it over some numbers on a blood test is terrifying but youre right they probably wont pull it they will just give me the short card and tell me to eat better which is what every doctor tells every trucker every time bondye bon im going to try to sleep now

That fear makes total sense — when your identity and income are tied to one card, any threat to it feels existential. But you're doing the right things, and March 28 is going to be fine. Take the pill, eat the oatmeal, skip the coffee that morning, and walk in there knowing you've got a plan.

Get some sleep. You've got this. Bondye bon, and goodnight.
